description: Residue number theory modelled on the residue subset of sympy.ntheory.residue_ntheory: legendreSymbol / isQuadraticResidue / jacobiSymbol / sqrtMod / nthrootMod / discreteLog / nOrder / totient. All values are discrete (Nat residues, Int symbol values in {-1,0,1}); there is no Float. The obligations follow the frozen-op unique-answer discipline: every output is characterized by an algebraic relation against Lean's immutable ^, %, *, ∣, ≤, <, and Nat.gcd over an unbounded numeric domain — never by agreement with a copyable reference body and never by a self-referential round-trip. Quadratic residuosity is pinned by a frozen existential over the complete residue system x < p (the iff carries both the soundness and completeness halves); the Legendre symbol is then pinned to {-1,0,1} by three mutually-exclusive iff's, the -1 case carrying the no-witness completeness clause. The three existence-bearing APIs — sqrtMod, nthrootMod, discreteLog — each carry ALL THREE of a soundness/witness clause, a least clause pinning the canonical representative, and a completeness clause (a returned none certifies no witness exists); the completeness half is mandatory so that the soundness clause alone is not satisfied by the degenerate fun _ _ _ => none (the explicit discrete_log anti-vacuity fix). The Jacobi symbol is anchored on its recurrence laws — base case (a/1)=1, numerator periodicity mod n, the value range, plus reciprocity check-points (agreement with Legendre at odd primes and concrete worked values) — rather than on a body the agent could copy, so the value is constrained while forcing the recurrence to be computed. nOrder is the least-positive-period characterization (paired witness + least clause); totient is anchored on frozen Nat.gcd as the unit count (base value, upper bound, positivity, and the value p-1 at a prime stated against frozen divisibility) and capped by Gauss's divisor-sum identity (the sum of totient over the positive divisors of n equals n, stated against a frozen divisorsOf enumeration) — a global law that no finite lookup table of totient values can satisfy. A further block of deep laws states clean end-facts whose proof needs a discovered structural argument the statement withholds: the multiplicative order divides exactly the exponents that return to 1 (a^k ≡ 1 mod n iff nOrder a n divides k) and equals 1 iff a ≡ 1; the discrete log is shift-invariant by the order (and by every multiple of it); and a square root's complement p-r is again a root with the canonical (least) root lying in the lower half (r ≤ p-r). Scope: the benchmark models the residue functions only — no factorisation or continued-fraction internals; symbols are meaningful at odd prime / odd n, the reference brute-force searches are bounded by p (a complete residue system), nOrder returns 0 as the no-order sentinel, and totient 0 = 0.
